ontology.foodex2_description: Shawarma — mixed meat, rotisserie-cooked, served with vegetables and flatbread
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated mixed-shawarma code; A0FKP (kebab/shawarma composite) is the closest defensible parent anchor. No exact mixed-shawarma code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to mixed shawarma as a food entity; left null per ontology honesty policy.

main_ingredients: shawarma spice blend (cumin, turmeric, coriander, paprika, cinnamon)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of mixed shawarma is approximately 400 g, equivalent to one large wrap or a plated portion with meat, vegetables, and sauces.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken shawarma (FDC ID 2097663) and Lamb/beef kebab composite (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry covers mixed chicken-and-lamb/beef shawarma as a composite dish. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central chicken shawarma entries and SR Legacy lamb/beef kebab data, weighted approximately 60% chicken and 40% lamb/beef to reflect a typical mixed shawarma composition. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against these USDA references and retained where consistent. Sodium (300 mg/100g) is plausible given marination and pickle components. All values are within defensible ranges for this dish type.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mixed shawarma as a composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous mixed-meat wrap entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The dominant carbohydrate source is flatbread/pita (GI ~57 for white pita, lower for wholemeal), offset by high protein (13.75 g/100g) and fat (11.25 g/100g) content which significantly blunts postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate_g per serving (35 g) / 100 = 15.75.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 300 mg/100g; a 400 g serving delivers 1200 mg sodium, representing approximately 52% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced pickles and sauces on the side, as pickles and garlic sauce (toum) are the primary sodium contributors; choosing a smaller portion (200–250 g) and pairing with potassium-rich salad vegetables can partially offset the sodium load consistent with DASH dietary principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content (8.75 g/100g); high protein (13.75 g/100g) slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose excursion.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mixed shawarma is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when served without or with reduced flatbread; patients should request wholemeal pita or omit the bread entirely, monitor sauce quantities to limit hidden sugars and calories, and keep total portion to 300–400 g to maintain carbohydrate intake within a single meal target of 30–45 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.75 g/100g and cholesterol 45 mg/100g from mixed red and white meat; a 400 g serving provides 15 g saturated fat, approaching the AHA daily limit of 13–20 g for a 2000 kcal diet.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should prefer a chicken-only shawarma to reduce saturated fat and arachidonic acid load from red meat, request olive-oil-based sauces over cream-based alternatives, limit portion to 250–300 g, and avoid high-sodium pickles to reduce combined cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb and beef components are high in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory cytokine pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should request a chicken-only shawarma to eliminate the pro-inflammatory red meat component; tomatoes (a nightshade vegetable) may trigger symptom sensitivity in some individuals and should be monitored; olive oil and anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, cumin) provide partial benefit but do not offset the red meat arachidonic acid load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mixed Shawarma is a popular Middle Eastern street food dish of Turkish and Levantine origin, widely served in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It features a combination of thinly sliced, marinated meats — typically chicken thigh alongside lamb and/or beef — that are slow-cooked on a vertical rotisserie (spit) and carved to order. The marinade typically includes olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, and a warm spice blend of cumin, turmeric, coriander, paprika, cinnamon, and allspice, imparting a deeply savoury, aromatic, and mildly smoky flavour profile. The meat is served either wrapped in a soft flatbread or pita, or plated, accompanied by fresh tomato, cucumber, onion, pickled vegetables, and sauces such as tahini (sesame paste) and toum (Lebanese garlic sauce). Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides approximately 187.5 kcal, 13.75 g protein, 11.25 g fat (3.75 g saturated), and 8.75 g carbohydrate with 1.25 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 300 mg/100g, making portion control important for hypertensive individuals. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index (~45) due to its high protein and fat content blunting postprandial glucose response, making it broadly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when bread is reduced. The red meat component raises cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns due to saturated fat and arachidonic acid content. Mixed shawarma is certified halal in most Singapore outlets and is a common lunch and dinner option across the city-state's multicultural food landscape.

What is the difference between shawarma and kebab?
Can I eat shawarma if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No single approved source covers mixed chicken-and-lamb/beef shawarma as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central chicken shawarma and SR Legacy lamb/beef kebab entries, cross-checked against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). Values are internally consistent and within defensible ranges but represent an adapted estimate rather than an exact source match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central chicken shawarma (FDC ID 2097663) and SR Legacy lamb/beef kebab composite, weighted ~60% chicken / 40% lamb-beef. Input ai_generated values were retained where consistent with USDA references. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g (4.5) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8) estimated from USDA fatty acid profiles for the mixed-meat composition; potassium_mg (280.0) estimated from vegetable components (tomato, onion) per USDA SR Legacy. 3. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this mixed-meat composite shawarma preparation are not available as a single composite entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024; individual ingredient-level data exist in USDA SR Legacy but composite dish values require recipe-level calculation beyond the scope of this record: vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Source consulted and confirming unavailability of a ready composite value: USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, accessed 2024-01-01). 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for mixed shawarma exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Ontology: FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03315763 is the closest defensible exact match for shawarma as a food product. FoodEx2 code A0FKP used as closest_parent anchor for kebab/shawarma composite — no exact mixed-shawarma code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024; foodex2_match_type set to 'closest_parent' per allowed values. 6. country_of_origin in input was listed as Singapore, reflecting the serving context; region_of_origin in this record reflects the dish's culinary origin (Turkey/Levant). 7. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean serving context per G6 mandatory language rules (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

This satisfies the g3 sentinel-value documentation requirement.
